title: Regulatory agencies
description: Practice understanding the role of government in banking, focusing on following governmental agencies: the Federal Reserve, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), National Credit Union Administration (NCUA).

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many laws and rules that financial institutions have to follow.

**Why are financial institutions regulated by the government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are many laws and rules that financial institutions have to follow.

**Why are financial institutions regulated by the government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to verify banks are following the rules

assessment_items.question_content:
There are many laws and rules that financial institutions have to follow.

**Why are financial institutions regulated by the government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to ensure banks are stable and can operate without problems

assessment_items.question_content:
Government agencies each have specific roles and responsibilities.

**Match each agency with its appropriate responsibility.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) hears complaints complaints.
The Federal Reserve (Fed) regulates the banking system.
The Office of the Comptroller of Currency (OCC) oversees national banks.
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) protects deposits.

Agency | Responsibility 
- | - | -
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) |  hear consumer complaints
The Federal Reserve (Fed) |  regulate the banking system
Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) | oversee national banks
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) | protect deposits

left: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)

assessment_items.question_content:
Government agencies each have specific roles and responsibilities.

**Match each agency with its appropriate responsibility.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) hears consumer complaints.
The Federal Reserve (Fed) regulates the banking system.
State regulators oversee banks in their specific state.
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) protects deposits.

Agency | Responsibility
- | - | -
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) | hear consumer complaints
The Federal Reserve (Fed) | regulate the banking system
state regulators| oversee banks in their state
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) | protect deposits

assessment_items.question_content:
Government agencies in the United States each have specific duties they are responsible for. 

**Match the agency to its correct responsibility.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: The Federal Reserve (Fed) oversees the banking system.
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) regulates national banks.
State regulators enforce state specific rules.
The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) takes consumer complaints.

Agency | Responsibility
- | - | -
The Federal Reserve (Fed) |oversee the banking system
Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) | regulate national banks
state regulators |enforce state specific rules
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) |  take consumer complaints

left: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)

assessment_items.question_content:
You've recently spotted some surprise charges on your bank account statement. You're certain these charges were not disclosed and you want to raise a formal complaint.

**Which agency should you contact to file a complaint against the bank for charging these fees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)

assessment_items.question_content:
Your bank has recently gone out of business and you are concerned that your money is gone.

**Which agency is responsible for returning you the funds that were on deposit at the bank? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)

assessment_items.question_content:
The credit union you bank at has recently closed and you would like to get your money back.

**Which regulatory agency will help you retrieve funds held by the closed credit union? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

You frequently use Venmo for daily expenses such as  sharing dinner bills and paying for rideshares. The last time you used the app it showed a balance of $\$150$. You soon discover unauthorized transactions and realize that a hacker accessed your account and took your entire balance.

**How much of the $\$150$ lost from your hacked Venmo account is protected by the [[☃ definition 1]]?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You use a money app for purchases at online stores and currently have $\$500$ in your account.  During the day news reports state the money app you use has gone out of business. 

**Which agency protects your money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You received a $\$100$ payment on CashApp that was later reversed for fraud. 

**How much of your money will be refunded by the  [[☃ definition 1]]?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
